heading: Determination of vessel delivery dates
label: 8671 Determination of vessel delivery dates

Certification, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–328, div. A, title I, § 121(b)130 Stat. 2030Dec. 23, 2016“(1).—In generalNot later than , the Secretary of the Navy shall certify to the congressional defense committees [Committees on Armed Services and Appropriations of the Senate and the House of Representatives] that the delivery dates of the following vessels have been adjusted in accordance with section 7301 [now 8671] of title 10, United States Code, as added by subsection (a):January 1, 2017“(A)The U.S.S. John F. Kennedy (CVN–79).“(B)The U.S.S. Zumwalt (DDG–1000).“(C)The U.S.S. Michael Monsoor (DDG–1001).“(D)The U.S.S. Lyndon B. Johnson (DDG–1002).“(E)Any other vessel of the Navy that is under construction on the date of the enactment of this Act [].Dec. 23, 2016“(2).—ContentsThe certification under paragraph (1) shall include—“(A)an identification of each vessel for which the delivery date was adjusted; and“(B)the delivery date of each such vessel, as so adjusted.”

children.text: the Secretary of the Navy determines that the vessel is assembled and complete; and
custody of the vessel and all systems contained in the vessel transfers to the Navy.

children.heading: .—Inclusion in Budget and Acquisition Reports
children.label: b .—Inclusion in Budget and Acquisition Reports

children.text: in the materials submitted to Congress by the Secretary of Defense in support of the budget of the President for each fiscal year (as submitted to Congress under , United States Code); andsection 1105(a) of title 31
in any relevant Selected Acquisition Report submitted to Congress under .section 4351 of this title

children.heading: .—Listing as Battle Force Ship in Naval Vessel Register
children.label: c .—Listing as Battle Force Ship in Naval Vessel Register
children.text: A covered vessel may not be listed in the Naval Vessel Register or other fleet inventory measures as a battle force ship until the delivery date specified in subsection (a).

The term “covered vessel” means any vessel of the Navy that is under construction or constructed using amounts authorized to be appropriated for the Department of Defense for shipbuilding and conversion, Navy.

children.label: A
children.text: A commissioned United States Ship warship capable of contributing to combat operations.

children.label: B
children.text: A United States Naval Ship that contributes directly to Navy warfighting or support missions.
